# fnmatch

```go
import "github.com/radiospiel/simple-go/fnmatch"
```

Package fnmatch provides shell\-style pattern matching \(fnmatch\) functionality. Patterns are converted to regular expressions.

## Index

- [Constants](<#constants>)
- [func Fnmatch\(pattern, path string, opts ...Options\) bool](<#Fnmatch>)

  - [func Compile\(pattern string, opts ...Options\) \(Matcher, error\)](<#Compile>)
  - [func MustCompile\(pattern string, opts ...Options\) Matcher](<#MustCompile>)
- [type Options](<#Options>)


## Constants

<a name="DefaultSeparators"></a>DefaultSeparators is used when no Options are provided. By default, \* and ? do not match "/" or "\\" \(i.e. Unix and Windows path separators.

```go
const DefaultSeparators = `/\`
```

<a name="Fnmatch"></a>
## func Fnmatch

```go
func Fnmatch(pattern, path string, opts ...Options) bool
```

Fnmatch checks if the path matches the fnmatch pattern. Options can be provided to customize matching behavior. When Separator is set, \* only matches characters except the separator.

<a name="Matcher"></a>
## type Matcher



```go
type Matcher interface {
    // MatchString reports whether the string s
    // contains any match of the regular expression re.
    MatchString(s string) bool
}
```

<a name="Compile"></a>
### func Compile

```go
func Compile(pattern string, opts ...Options) (Matcher, error)
```

Compile compiles an fnmatch pattern and returns a Matcher. Options can be provided to customize matching behavior. When no Options are provided, DefaultSeparators \("/\\"\) is used. To match any character with \*, pass Options\{Separators: ""\}. Results are cached based on \(pattern, separators\) to avoid repeated compilation.

<a name="MustCompile"></a>
### func MustCompile

```go
func MustCompile(pattern string, opts ...Options) Matcher
```

MustCompile compiles an fnmatch pattern and returns a Matcher. Options can be provided to customize matching behavior. panics on error

<a name="Options"></a>
## type Options

Options configures fnmatch behavior.

```go
type Options struct {
    // Separators is a string where each character acts as a separator.
    // When empty, * matches any character. When set (e.g., "/."),
    // * only matches characters that are not separators.
    // For example, with Separators: "/.", "*" matches neither "/" nor ".".
    // Default (when no Options provided): "/\" - see DefaultSeparators.
    // To match any character, explicitly pass Options{Separators: ""}.
    Separators string
}
```
